summary: Iteration 29: Interview Schedule Reports performance fix + Update Scores pagination + back button rollout. Backend: 12/12 pytest tests PASS in 24s on /api/bb/interview-reports (pagination, filters, sort, summary), /api/bb/attended-for-scores (pagination, filters), and PUT /api/bb/applicant-score. Frontend: All 12 protected pages show data-testid='back-btn'; /home and /login do NOT. /interview-reports loads with Page 1 of 50 (4919 records), summary tiles non-zero (1024 attended / 3895 not / 948 premium / 3971 non-premium). /update-scores has pagination + modal pre-populates status & scores; page-size change works. No blocking issues.

interview_reports_api: GET /api/bb/interview-reports?page=1&limit=10 -> 200 in <10s, total=4919, totalPages=492, all required keys present (data,total,page,limit,totalPages,summary{role_counts,attended,not_attended,premium_colleges,non_premium_colleges})
Filter collegeType=Premium + attendance=Attended -> only Premium College + Attended rows (no leak)
Filter collegeType=Non Premium -> only Non Premium College rows
Filter attendance=Not Attended -> only Not Attended rows
Filter jobRole=<top role from summary> -> only that role's rows
page=1 vs page=2 returns DISTINCT rows; total & totalPages constant
Sort by schedule_date DESC verified on first 3 rows
attended_for_scores_api: page=1 vs page=2 with limit=50 returns DISTINCT rows; total constant
applicant_score_put: Verified persisted via subsequent GET (status updated, TEST round score=99.5 present)
Reverted status to original 'On hold'; cleaned up test scores via direct DB delete on bb_applicant_updates (since PUT cannot clear scores; see code review note)
frontend_back_button: Click back-btn from /summary navigated to previous /update-scores page (history-based nav works)
frontend_interview_reports_ui: Page loads, summary tiles show non-zero (1024/3895/948/3971)
Pagination footer renders: 'Page 1 of 50 (4919 records)' with prev/next caret buttons (no testids but functional)
Filters (job role, attendance, college, date) and APPLY/Reset/Export buttons render
frontend_update_scores_ui: data-testid='pagination', 'page-size-select', 'next-page-btn' all present on bottom of /update-scores
Page-size change to '50/page' reloads table with 50 rows (DB-paged)
Update buttons (100 visible) trigger 'Update Score' modal that pre-populates status='On hold' and existing scores (verified 99.5 prefill from prior test data)
Cancel closes modal; pagination state preserved

critical_code_review_comments: InterviewReports.js pagination prev/next buttons (lines ~155-160) lack data-testid attributes. UpdateScores has them ('pagination','page-size-select','prev-page-btn','next-page-btn') — recommend adding equivalent testids to InterviewReports for testability parity.

PUT /api/bb/applicant-score/{email} cannot CLEAR existing scores because handler uses `if data.scores:` (empty list is falsy). To erase test scores I had to delete the bb_applicant_updates row directly. Consider treating `scores=[]` as explicit clear (use `if data.scores is not None`).

In get_attended_for_scores (bb_modules.py line ~507-510), the date-range merge `match['schedule_date'] = {**match.get('schedule_date',{}),...}` overwrites the base `{$nin:..., $exists:True}` — works but fragile; use `match['schedule_date'].update(...)` after seeding the dict.

Interview reports endpoint optimization is well-implemented: aggregation pipelines on persisted _normalized_job_role + _nirf_category + DB-level $skip/$limit. Confirmed performant (<10s for total=4919; first request 1.6s). Summary aggregations run over the matched set (correct contract).
available_rounds in /attended-for-scores is computed via score_sheet.distinct('round_name') on EVERY page request — could cache (5-min TTL) since global set rarely changes.
seed_data_creation: Single PUT used a real email (ajithkumarm0607@gmail.com) — value reverted in test teardown; leftover scores on bb_applicant_updates cleaned up via direct DB delete (1 doc removed). registered_candidates result_status reset check ran (0 modified, no leak). Production data otherwise untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration29_pagination.py — covers /api/bb/interview-reports (pagination/filters/sort/summary), /api/bb/attended-for-scores (pagination/filters), and PUT /api/bb/applicant-score. All tests login with 'Admin User'/'Admin User'. PUT test mutates a REAL email's status briefly then reverts; remember PUT cannot clear scores so any added scores need DB cleanup if they leak. InterviewReports.js still lacks data-testid on prev/next pagination buttons — re-check after any refactor. Backend interview-reports response time: ~1.6s on first call, <1s subsequent (with cached _normalized_job_role / _nirf_category indices).

mocked_apis.WhatsApp AiSensy: Returns 401 - unchanged, not exercised in iter29
